review.correction_note: TIA section 187N(3) requires records until the statutory review is completed. It is relevant retention evidence but no numeric period is stated, so it supports score 0 rather than score 1. [Final joint-session workbook, 20 Jul — supersedes any prior batch entry.]

review.reviewer_role: Independent Legal Consultant, Legal Review, zAI BD
review.correction_note: Telecommunications Act Schedule 1 clause 41(1) imposes an ongoing duty to keep overhead-line records. It has no numeric minimum and must be treated only as score-0 P7-I3 evidence. [Final joint-session workbook, 20 Jul — supersedes any prior batch entry.]

review.reviewer_role: Independent Legal Consultant, Legal Review, zAI BD
review.correction_note: Telecommunications Act Schedule 1 clause 41(2) imposes an ongoing duty to keep transmission-tower records. It has no numeric period and must be treated only as score-0 evidence. [Final joint-session workbook, 20 Jul — supersedes any prior batch entry.]

review.reviewer_role: Independent Legal Consultant, Legal Review, zAI BD
review.correction_note: Section 408(6) merely permits a later instrument to specify a retention period; it neither imposes the duty itself nor states the period. Without the operative instrument and numeric duration it is not a usable P7-I3 finding. [Final joint-session workbook, 20 Jul — supersedes any prior batch entry.]

review.reviewer_role: Independent Legal Consultant, Legal Review, zAI BD
review.correction_note: Section 150(1) allows an officer to ask for documents only after consensual entry; it does not compel production. The rationale incorrectly says 'require'. A voluntary request is not a statutory requirement allowing non-judicial access for this indicator. [Final joint-session workbook, 20 Jul — supersedes any prior batch entry.]

review.reviewer_role: Independent Legal Consultant, Legal Review, zAI BD
review.correction_note: AMLA section 167(2) lets an authorised officer compel information, documents or copies by written notice without independent judicial approval. It directly satisfies P7-I5. [Final joint-session workbook, 20 Jul — supersedes any prior batch entry.]
